entries.title: No Fallback AI Decisions
entries.text: No fallback, default, random, coerced, or substitute move is allowed for an AI player. Invalid, malformed, process-error, or timeout output is rejected and audited. Mandatory AI action decisions can block the game rather than auto-choosing a move.

entries.title: Custom Financial Instruments Extend Classic Trading
entries.text: Players may negotiate structured instruments that do not exist in classic Monopoly, including installment loans, collateralized debt, purchase options, insurance payouts, guarantees, conditional obligations, default penalties, and rent-share obligations. These agreements are only executable after backend validation creates a contract.

entries.title: Deterministic Negotiation Cutoffs
entries.text: Negotiations can expire through deterministic cutoff rules for rounds, proposals, pending offers, wall-clock waits, or AI response opportunities. Expiration closes the negotiation without creating a fallback action or changing ownership by implication.
